# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a React application. Install Node.js, run npm install, build the production bundle with npm run build, and serve it with a lightweight HTTP server. Keep the Dockerfile simple and easy to understand for our team.
# Category: container_security

# Set the base image as node
FROM node:14-alpine

# Create app directory
WORKDIR /usr/src/app

# Copy package.json and package-lock.json to current directory 
COPY package*.json ./

# Install dependencies with npm install
RUN npm install

# Copy the rest of our app source code from our local machine into our container
COPY . .

# Build production bundle using npm run build command
RUN npm run build

# Expose port 3000 for use outside this docker environment.
EXPOSE 3000

# Use the lightweight http-server as a static server for our app. 
CMD ["npx", "http-server", "-p", "3000", "build"]
